textByHashes.bd1e34f57ecc2d7eba2b5847423177d8f4a6b8b3: Well, friend, I'm Arls Lutwidge of the Moonlight Broadsword! Renowned all over Gielinor!
textByHashes.8184c4f97857641425c8a8b5c313057ca1aaafa6: You haven't... heard of... That's madness!
textByHashes.8e4c03733db623dc2e659244436cda59deef3adc: Surely you've heard of my feats, but perhaps not the name?
textByHashes.bd4dc36bdcaba7933e5d316636f8acc650d47c25: Very good, young one! Very good indeed!
textByHashes.5e243562cd879c69b8bff5ca7566582ac9ba7634: Gone. I gave it to a fledgling when I retired and the damned fool went and died fighting some great monster in a far off land.
textByHashes.b9e13f17ed4e461d6cf3c252e2d8fd71043671a9: Say... if you find it, could you bring it to me? I won't pay you, as holding such a magnificent weapon should be payment enough.
textByHashes.bebd11053af5a3f6fbb1a2299a4fe33a6a959442: I peaked rather early and most know me for my first act of heroism when I defeated the Moonfly. It may sound cute, but it killed many of the smallfolk and their livestock, for it was a vile creature.
textByHashes.a8f06eb8a68d3851df339e1e9a3ec24686249fbe: It was I that discovered the creature. I pulled it from the Lum during a fishing trip of mine.
textByHashes.6ccdc064bcc008707bd46aef4f0471b6bf997b98: As young and foolish as I was, I dropped it down a dried up well and went for help. However, upon my return, the creature was gone.
textByHashes.1d0d5e91ffbc212a6990ba1417f1ba822db952fc: Many years passed until suddenly there were sightings of a winged demon around the River Lum. Some claimed it was an overly large heron and that it was nothing to be worried about.
textByHashes.cc4da04eb7e69cb22916a48a926cdeebc5a6b06c: As time went on, folk started to go missing and I knew I had to return home and face this demon.
textByHashes.0650831a6e880fb30cb28d47872c5dfd511e217f: I had wars to fight, glory to be won and all that rubbish. Upon my return I spoke to an old crone who lived in the swamps and she gave me some advice.
textByHashes.bb3f978edb9355b24393a2c1f726daef4c1067d7: She told me to spike my armour and described how to lure the creature toward me.
textByHashes.b356db22b8726696d8fc3f4e54e8a60abb1be7a3: Some bard wrote a poem of the events. The telling of the battle is a little embellished and rife with errors. The poem can be found in this library somewhere, providing it has been returned.
textByHashes.b04b408622d649ce1361ad7f6d9239f589600cbf: And did I defeat the creature? I wouldn't be here if I hadn't!
textByHashes.adcdbef8d46d0df84009b721fe9a63c65b1e27bb: Please do! And come to me if you have any burning questions about it.
textByHashes.f7d39a6eb61d0ac4be6a5ccba6cf50e69694cfd5: I've grown old, friend. Heroes need to know when to throw in the towel, so they say. I've always enjoyed a good book, so it seemed fitting to stay here as librarian for the guild.
textByHashes.e246a9688ef88f4f1cc4dff478f50f5650271f71: Well, that's why I'm here! So many of you adventurers help yourself to any old book and never return the damn things!
textByHashes.d4f5b0dadfe061a4e562b6114ce21bcdcd4496a9: If I could have it my way, you wouldn't be allowed to wander off with them, but I'm not the guildmaster, as Alec likes to remind me.
textByHashes.95fcb974ef4fe27183d7a36fa568a92c6f4e3a0f: Well fear not. I'm working on it!
textByHashes.873379abe6f1234fc5c3a82ccaf7f299d8d26e77: Yes! Fantastic! Did you enjoy it?
textByHashes.fc5772e30d687a1dcb28311a9ebdff97f65c19a0: Ah, a slight misunderstanding... I soaked it in sap from a yew tree, but I guess blood is a bit more dramatic.
textByHashes.61990bf2ac3711e49f8ed027b46960928d8853a7: The crone, long dead now, I suppose... She claimed to be from the same world as the creature and knew of the ritual to summon it.
textByHashes.42d0fdb283361f781419e9d65715f5184f1568b2: Perhaps some locals claimed to have lost some sheep, but my guess is that the bard was searching for a rhyme. Either way, I doubt it's a detail worth fretting over.
